title: Questions for Maggie Cnossen
excerpt: Do you remember your childhood dreams? Yes, I remember flying over a field of wild horses while they were running.
body:
Do you remember your childhood dreams?
Yes, I remember flying over a field of wild horses while they were running. And I had these recurring dreams where I was hovering above myself, almost observing myself sleep.

Morning shower or evening?
Evening

Is there anything you regret not doing?
A lot of photos I regret not taking.

What was your first job?
My first job was at a Ryan Sheckler charity golf tournament at Trump National in Palos Verdes. I was fifteen, on summer break in San Diego, and my friend's mom drove us up. We went around the course checking on guests and selling golf balls for a hundred dollars each. At the end of the day, they dropped all the balls out of a helicopter over the last hole, like some rich-people lottery. Whoever's ball landed in the hole won a prize. I remember selling five to T-Pain and asking him how to spell his name. I made three hundred dollars that day.

What would you put on your tombstone?
Here lies someone who almost left early.

What would be your last meal?
I don't want to exit on a full stomach.

What are you reading?
The Loser by Thomas Bernhard

What movie have you seen the most times?
10 Things I Hate About You

Thoughts on love?
This line always stuck with me out of Gravity and Grace by Simone Weil. "I do not love you in order for you to love me back; I love you so that you may love yourself."

What are your beauty secrets?
less is more

How do you know when you've had enough?
When I start to feel guilt

When do you get nervous?
When I care

What's living on your nightstand?
Jewelry, journal, mail, and a candle

Favorite thing about the city you live in?
It doesn't have to feel like a city if you don't want it to.

What makes you smile?
The ocean

Have you been kicked out anywhere?
Sunset Tower

What keeps you here?
I'm not worthy of leaving yet.
